01 / About
Architecting AI/ML Systems.

I build AI/ML systems that move from ambiguous problem → architecture → model/retrieval logic → deployed product. The throughline is simple: use machine learning, LLMs, and reliable data pipelines to solve operational problems people actually use.

My work spans production-style RAG systems, agentic research workflows, healthcare prediction pipelines, and enterprise decision systems. I pair an M.S. in Statistics & Analytics from UNC Chapel Hill with hands-on delivery across pharma supply chains, MS patient journeys, and deployed AI demos.

What I work on: LLM applications with hybrid retrieval, guardrails, and evaluation · machine-learning pipelines for forecasting, classification, and treatment-switch prediction · solution architecture that connects data, model, API, UX, and stakeholder workflow · production-minded delivery where reliability matters as much as accuracy.

What makes me different: I can translate between engineers, operators, and decision-makers. I have shipped live AI systems, designed models with measurable lift, presented to senior stakeholders, and taught ML fundamentals to 140+ students. I do not just build models — I turn them into usable systems.

Open to AI/ML engineer, machine learning engineer, solutions engineer, solutions architect, applied ML, and forward-deployed engineer roles where I can build technical solutions end to end.

Let's connect →

AI/ML Systems Philosophy
01
🧭
Problem-First Architecture
I start with the workflow, failure mode, and user decision — then choose the right mix of ML, retrieval, API, and interface.
02
🧠
Models With Judgment
AUC is not enough. I care about calibration, lift, confidence thresholds, human review, and whether the model can be trusted in the loop.
03
⚙️
Production-Minded Pipelines
Good AI systems need reliable data paths. I design pipelines from ingestion through features, retrieval, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring.
04
🤝
Solution Delivery
The best technical system still has to land with humans. I translate complex models into clear workflows for operators, leaders, and product teams.

02 / Experience
Career Path
Data Analyst
Viatris
Internship · Remote
May 2025 — May 2026
Designed decision systems across 165+ markets to identify stockout risk, planning inefficiencies, and escalation paths within a $15B pharmaceutical portfolio.
Built 5+ production-facing intelligence products used by 100+ leaders — ranking top 5% company-wide for adoption and enabling executive supply-chain decisions.
Reduced inactive POs by 88% and supported a 24–25% reduction in supply gap value across OSD Internal and Emerging Markets.
Architected repeatable Alteryx + SQL + Power BI pipelines that turned messy planning data into reliable, reusable decision workflows.

Healthcare Data Scientist
NMSS
Part Time · Remote
Aug 2025 — May 2026
Analyzed 10+ years of U.S. medical claims covering 1M+ MS patients — generating real-world evidence on treatment adoption and switching behavior.
Engineered longitudinal datasets integrating diagnosis, pharmacy, and procedure claims — enabling end-to-end patient care pathway analysis.
Found 50–60% of treatment switches driven by generic substitution — a key insight into real-world prescribing behavior at population scale.
Built a 3-stage ML pipeline (18K+ patients, 31K+ episodes, ICD-10 / pharmacy cohorts) for therapy initiation, switch probability, and next-line decisions.
Achieved AUC 0.95 on switch prediction with an 8× lift on top-decile risk identification — actionable for high-risk patient targeting.
Next-line therapy prediction at 57.8% group / 36.7% molecule accuracy — outperforming baselines.

03 / Projects
Selected Works
HybridRAG Classifier
Active
6-label FDA compliance classifier combining BGE dense embeddings (pgvector/HNSW) and BM25 sparse retrieval via Reciprocal Rank Fusion. Routes low-confidence outputs (<70%) to a human review queue. Weekly GitHub Actions scraping pipeline + LLM gateway security layer with input sanitization and rate limiting.
Python
RAG
HuggingFace
RAGAS
Docker
↗ Live site
PRISM
Active
Multi-document research-intelligence pipeline. BM25 + dense hybrid retrieval with RRF fusion. Qdrant vector store. Structured intelligence briefs via Groq LLM (JSON mode). Cross-document conflict detection. LangGraph orchestration in progress.
LangGraph
Qdrant
BM25
FastAPI
Groq
↗ Diagram
PULSE — Agentic Search & Intelligence Pipeline
Active
Parallel multi-agent retrieval system with Router → 3 concurrent research agents (News · Sentiment · Trends) → Synthesis. FastAPI backend streams real-time progress via SSE. Input/output guardrail stack with LLM self-correction retry loop. Pure SVG/CSS client-side dashboard.
FastAPI
Anthropic API
Multi-agent
SSE
Tavily
↗ Diagram
Treatment Switching Dynamics — NMSS DMT Pipeline
Live
3-stage ML pipeline predicting therapy initiation, switch probability, and next-line treatment decisions on managed-care claims. AUC 0.95 on switch prediction with 8× lift on top-decile risk identification. Next-line therapy at 57.8% group / 36.7% molecule accuracy — outperforming baselines.

Healthcare Demand Forecasting — Labcorp
Completed
Hybrid ML + time-series forecasting system (Gradient Boosting + ARIMA) projecting diagnostic-test demand through 2026 across specialties. Modeled adoption, timing, and repeat-utilization separately to correct early-stage data bias and prevent misleading demand spikes. Engineered a working-days adjustment to isolate true demand from calendar variation for capacity planning.
ARIMA
Gradient Boosting
Python
Time Series
Healthcare
↗ LinkedIn Post
CO₂ Time Series Forecasting (SARIMA)
Completed
Predicted atmospheric CO₂ levels from Mauna Loa historical data using SARIMA. Seasonal decomposition, ACF/PACF diagnostics, and grid search over AIC to tune optimal parameters — forecasts with confidence intervals capturing the long-term upward trend.

Publications
5 articles on Medium · Towards AI & Generative AI publications.
I Thought MCP Was Just Another Name for Function Calling. I Was Wrong.
Medium · 2026
Why the Model Context Protocol is architecturally distinct from plain function calling — server-side state, bi-directional control, and what that means for real agent systems.
MCP
Agents
LLMs
↗ Read on Medium
Why HybridRAG Changed The Way I Think About AI Systems
Medium · 2026
How building a production RAG classifier forced a rethink of retrieval — why neither dense nor sparse alone is enough, and how RRF fusion changes the outcome at the claim level.
RAG
BGE
BM25
RRF
↗ Read on Medium
How Chemical Engineering Made Data Pipelines Feel Familiar
Medium · 2026
Process control, mass balances, and unit operations as mental models for ETL, streaming pipelines, and data quality at scale.
Data Eng.
ETL
Systems
↗ Read on Medium
AI Kept Forgetting My Notes — Fixing That Taught Me How It Actually Works
Medium · 2026
A first-principles walkthrough of how LLM context windows and retrieval behave once you push them past trivial demos — what fails, why, and how to design around it.
LLMs
RAG
Context
↗ Read on Medium
Linear Regression Rarely Fails. It Drifts.
Medium · 2026
Why production ML systems fail less from wrong model and more from drift — covariate, concept, label. Practical monitoring patterns (PSI, KS, calibration plots) and retraining triggers that don't oscillate.
MLOps
Drift
Monitoring
↗ Read on Medium
